text: OpenAI Global, LLC is an American artificial intelligence research organization headquartered in San Francisco. The company was established in December 2015 by Elon Musk, Sam Altman, Ilya Sutskever, Greg Brockman, and seven other co-founders, with an initial $1 billion in pledged capital. The organization developed the GPT family of large language models, the DALL-E series for text-to-image generation, and the Sora text-to-video models. ChatGPT was released in November 2022. Microsoft has invested over $13 billion in OpenAI and provides Azure cloud computing resources. Sam Altman serves as CEO of OpenAI.

Google LLC is an American multinational technology corporation specializing in search engines, online advertising, cloud computing, and artificial intelligence. Founded in 1998 by Larry Page and Sergey Brin while they were PhD students at Stanford University. In 2015, Google reorganized as a subsidiary of Alphabet Inc., its parent holding company. Sundar Pichai serves as CEO of Google since 2015 and also became CEO of Alphabet in 2019. Google offers products including Gmail, Google Maps, Chrome, YouTube, Android, and the Gemini AI model.

Meta Platforms, Inc. is an American multinational technology corporation established on February 4, 2004, in Cambridge, Massachusetts. Originally launched as TheFacebook, Inc., the company rebranded to Facebook, Inc. in 2005 before adopting its current name Meta in 2021. Founded by Mark Zuckerberg alongside Eduardo Saverin, Dustin Moskovitz, Andrew McCollum, and Chris Hughes, Meta operates Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, Messenger, and Threads. Meta is headquartered in Menlo Park, California. Mark Zuckerberg serves as CEO of Meta.

Apple Inc. is an American multinational technology corporation based in Cupertino, California. Founded on April 1, 1976, by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne. The firm rebranded from Apple Computer, Inc. to Apple Inc. in 2007. Apple's products include the iPhone, iPad, Mac, Apple Watch, and AirPods, plus services like Apple Pay, iCloud, Apple Music, and Apple TV+. Tim Cook assumed the CEO position in 2011 following Steve Jobs' resignation.

Microsoft Corporation was founded on April 4, 1975, in Albuquerque, New Mexico, by Bill Gates and Paul Allen. Microsoft is headquartered in Redmond, Washington. The company makes the Windows operating system, the Office productivity suite, the Azure cloud platform, the Bing search engine, and the Xbox gaming platform. Microsoft acquired LinkedIn in 2016, GitHub in 2018 for $7.5 billion, and Activision Blizzard in 2023 for $68.7 billion. Satya Nadella has served as CEO of Microsoft since 2014.

Nvidia Corporation was established on April 5, 1993, by Jensen Huang, Chris Malachowsky, and Curtis Priem. The company is headquartered in Santa Clara, California. Nvidia designs graphics processing units (GPUs) including the GeForce consumer line and the RTX professional line. Nvidia developed CUDA, a parallel computing platform and API. Jensen Huang serves as CEO of Nvidia.

Anthropic was founded in 2021 and is an American artificial intelligence company headquartered in San Francisco. The company was established by seven former OpenAI employees, including siblings Dario Amodei and Daniela Amodei. Dario Amodei serves as CEO and Daniela Amodei serves as president. The flagship product is Claude, a series of large language models released initially in March 2023. Amazon invested $4 billion in Anthropic in 2023, followed by an additional $4 billion in November 2024. Google invested $500 million in Anthropic in October 2023.

DeepMind was established in November 2010 by Demis Hassabis, Shane Legg, and Mustafa Suleyman. The company is headquartered in London. DeepMind was acquired by Google in 2014. In April 2023, DeepMind merged with Google Brain to form Google DeepMind, operating as a subsidiary of Alphabet. DeepMind developed AlphaGo, which defeated Go champion Lee Sedol in 2016, AlphaZero, AlphaFold for protein structure prediction, and contributes to the Gemini language model.

YouTube was founded on February 14, 2005, by three former PayPal employees: Chad Hurley, Steve Chen, and Jawed Karim. The first video, titled 'Me at the zoo', was uploaded on April 23, 2005, by Jawed Karim. Google acquired YouTube on November 13, 2006, for $1.65 billion in stock. YouTube is headquartered in San Bruno, California.

GitHub is a collaborative software development platform established by Tom Preston-Werner, Chris Wanstrath, P.J. Hyett, and Scott Chacon. Development commenced in October 2007 and the service officially launched in April 2008. Microsoft acquired GitHub on June 4, 2018, for $7.5 billion in stock, with the deal closing on October 26, 2018. GitHub operates as a subsidiary of Microsoft.

Tesla, Inc. is an American automotive and clean energy company headquartered in Austin, Texas. Tesla was founded on July 1, 2003, by Martin Eberhard and Marc Tarpenning. In February 2004, Elon Musk led Tesla's first funding round with a $6.5 million investment, becoming chairman. Elon Musk assumed the role of CEO in October 2008. Tesla's products include the Roadster, Model S, Model X, Model 3, Model Y, Semi, and Cybertruck. Tesla acquired SolarCity in 2016.

Instagram is an American photo and short-form video sharing social network owned by Meta Platforms. The platform was created by Kevin Systrom and Mike Krieger. Instagram officially launched on October 12, 2010. In April 2012, Facebook (now Meta Platforms) acquired Instagram for approximately $1 billion in combined cash and stock. The acquisition closed on September 6, 2012.

WhatsApp is a messaging application founded in 2009 by Brian Acton and Jan Koum, both former Yahoo! employees. WhatsApp is headquartered in Menlo Park, California. Facebook (now Meta Platforms) acquired WhatsApp in February 2014 for approximately $19 billion in cash and stock, the largest acquisition in Facebook's history at that time.

LinkedIn is a business and employment-focused social network founded in December 2002 by Reid Hoffman, Allen Blue, Konstantin Guericke, Eric Ly, and Jean-Luc Vaillant. LinkedIn officially launched on May 5, 2003. Microsoft acquired LinkedIn in December 2016 for $26.2 billion. LinkedIn operates as a subsidiary of Microsoft and is headquartered in Sunnyvale, California.

ChatGPT is an AI chatbot developed by OpenAI, released on November 30, 2022. ChatGPT is built on the GPT family of large language models. ChatGPT became the fastest-growing consumer software application in history, gaining over 100 million users within two months of release. ChatGPT runs on Microsoft Azure cloud infrastructure.

Claude is a family of large language models developed by Anthropic, first released in March 2023. Anthropic developed Claude. Claude is named after Claude Shannon. Claude competes with OpenAI's GPT models and Google's Gemini. Claude is available through Anthropic's API and through Amazon Bedrock.

AlphaGo is a computer program developed by DeepMind that plays the board game Go. AlphaGo defeated Lee Sedol, an 18-time world champion, in March 2016 in a five-game match held in Seoul, South Korea. AlphaGo was the first computer program to defeat a professional human Go player without handicaps.

AlphaFold is an artificial intelligence program developed by DeepMind that predicts protein 3D structures from amino acid sequences. AlphaFold 2 was released in 2020 and revolutionized structural biology. Demis Hassabis and John Jumper of DeepMind received the 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for AlphaFold.

SolarCity was a solar energy services company founded in 2006 by Lyndon Rive and Peter Rive, cousins of Elon Musk. Tesla acquired SolarCity in November 2016 for $2.6 billion. SolarCity is now part of Tesla Energy.

PayPal is an online payments company founded in December 1998 by Max Levchin, Peter Thiel, and Luke Nosek as Confinity. Confinity merged with X.com, founded by Elon Musk, in March 2000, and the merged company was renamed PayPal in 2001. eBay acquired PayPal in October 2002 for $1.5 billion.

The PayPal Mafia refers to early PayPal employees and founders who later created influential technology companies. Steve Chen co-founded YouTube. Chad Hurley co-founded YouTube. Jawed Karim co-founded YouTube. Elon Musk co-founded Tesla and SpaceX. Reid Hoffman co-founded LinkedIn. Peter Thiel co-founded Palantir. Max Levchin founded Affirm. David Sacks co-founded Yammer.

Alphabet Inc. is an American multinational technology conglomerate founded on October 2, 2015, through a corporate restructuring of Google. Alphabet is headquartered in Mountain View, California. Larry Page served as CEO of Alphabet from 2015 until December 2019, when Sundar Pichai took over. Alphabet's subsidiaries include Google, YouTube, DeepMind, Waymo, and Verily.

Waymo is an autonomous driving technology company and subsidiary of Alphabet Inc. Waymo started as the Google Self-Driving Car Project in 2009 and became a standalone Alphabet subsidiary in December 2016. Waymo is headquartered in Mountain View, California.

xAI is an American artificial intelligence company founded by Elon Musk in March 2023. xAI is headquartered in the San Francisco Bay Area. The company developed the Grok large language model. xAI acquired the social media platform X (formerly Twitter) in March 2025. Elon Musk founded xAI.

SpaceX is an American space technology company founded by Elon Musk in March 2002. SpaceX is headquartered in Hawthorne, California. SpaceX develops the Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy rockets, the Dragon spacecraft, and the Starship vehicle. Gwynne Shotwell serves as President and COO of SpaceX. Elon Musk founded SpaceX.

GPT-4 is a large multimodal language model developed by OpenAI, released on March 14, 2023. GPT-4 is the successor to GPT-3.5 and powers the paid version of ChatGPT. GPT-4 was trained on Microsoft Azure infrastructure.

Gemini is a family of large language models developed by Google DeepMind, first announced in December 2023. Gemini is the successor to LaMDA and PaLM 2. Gemini powers Google's Bard chatbot, which was rebranded to Gemini in February 2024.

CUDA (Compute Unified Device Architecture) is a parallel computing platform and API model created by Nvidia in 2007. CUDA allows software developers to use Nvidia GPUs for general-purpose processing. CUDA is widely used in deep learning frameworks like PyTorch and TensorFlow.

PyTorch is an open-source machine learning framework originally developed by Meta AI Research, first released in September 2016. In September 2022, PyTorch was transferred to the PyTorch Foundation under the Linux Foundation. PyTorch is widely used for deep learning research and production.

TensorFlow is an open-source machine learning framework developed by Google Brain, first released on November 9, 2015. TensorFlow is used for both research and production deployments at Google in products like Search, Gmail, and Photos.

Amazon.com, Inc. is an American multinational technology company founded by Jeff Bezos on July 5, 1994, in Bellevue, Washington. Amazon is headquartered in Seattle, Washington. Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the cloud computing subsidiary of Amazon, launched in 2006. Andy Jassy succeeded Jeff Bezos as CEO of Amazon in July 2021.

Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the largest investor in Anthropic. AWS provides cloud infrastructure for training Anthropic's Claude models on AWS Trainium chips. Claude models are also available through Amazon Bedrock, AWS's foundation model service.

X, formerly known as Twitter, is a social media platform founded by Jack Dorsey, Noah Glass, Biz Stone, and Evan Williams in March 2006. Elon Musk acquired Twitter in October 2022 for $44 billion and rebranded it to X in July 2023. xAI acquired X in March 2025.

Oculus VR is a virtual reality technology company founded by Palmer Luckey in July 2012. Facebook (now Meta Platforms) acquired Oculus in March 2014 for $2 billion. Oculus is now part of Meta's Reality Labs division and produces the Meta Quest line of VR headsets.

Jeff Bezos established Amazon on July 5, 1994. Amazon maintains dual headquarters in Seattle, Washington, and Arlington County, Virginia. The company owns over 100 subsidiaries, including Amazon Web Services, Whole Foods Market (acquired 2017 for $13.7 billion), Twitch (live-streaming platform), Amazon MGM Studios, Zappos, Audible, Kuiper Systems (satellite internet), and Ring.

International Business Machines Corporation (IBM) was established in 1911 by Charles Ranlett Flint, Herman Hollerith, and George Winthrop Fairchild. IBM is headquartered in Armonk, New York. IBM developed Watson, an AI system that defeated human Jeopardy! champions in 2011. IBM acquired Red Hat in October 2018 for $34 billion.

Intel Corporation was established on July 18, 1968, in Mountain View, California, by Gordon Moore and Robert Noyce alongside investor Arthur Rock. Intel is headquartered in Santa Clara, California. Intel designs CPUs including the Intel Core family. Intel created the first commercial microprocessor, the Intel 4004, in 1971. Intel co-developed the x86 instruction set.

Salesforce, Inc. is a cloud-based software company headquartered in San Francisco. Salesforce was founded in March 1999 by Marc Benioff, Parker Harris, Dave Moellenhoff, and Frank Dominguez. Marc Benioff was a former Oracle executive. Larry Ellison was an early investor. Salesforce acquired MuleSoft for $6.5 billion in 2018, Tableau Software for $15.7 billion in 2019, and Slack Technologies for $27.7 billion in 2021.

Oracle Corporation was co-founded in 1977 by Larry Ellison, Bob Miner, and Ed Oates in Santa Clara, California. Oracle is headquartered in Austin, Texas. Oracle acquired Sun Microsystems in 2010 for over $7 billion, gaining the Java programming language. Oracle acquired Cerner Corporation in 2022 for $28.3 billion. Oracle also acquired PeopleSoft and Siebel Systems.

Adobe Inc. was established in December 1982 by John Warnock and Charles Geschke. Adobe is headquartered in San Jose, California. Adobe's flagship products include Photoshop, Illustrator, and Acrobat. Acrobat introduced the PDF format in 1993. Adobe acquired Macromedia in 2005. In September 2022, Adobe announced a $20 billion acquisition of Figma; this deal was abandoned in December 2023 with Adobe paying a $1 billion termination fee.

Spotify was established in April 2006 in Stockholm, Sweden by Daniel Ek and Martin Lorentzon. Daniel Ek was formerly chief technology officer at Stardoll. Martin Lorentzon was co-founder of Tradedoubler. Spotify is a music streaming platform headquartered in Stockholm.

Netflix was established by Reed Hastings and Marc Randolph on August 29, 1997, in Scotts Valley, California. Netflix is headquartered in Los Gatos, California. Netflix launched its streaming service in 2007.

Uber Technologies was founded in March 2009 by Travis Kalanick and Garrett Camp. Garrett Camp was a co-founder of StumbleUpon. Uber is headquartered in San Francisco. Dara Khosrowshahi assumed the role of CEO of Uber in August 2017, replacing Travis Kalanick. Khosrowshahi was previously CEO of Expedia Group.

Airbnb, Inc. was established in August 2008 by Brian Chesky, Joe Gebbia, and Nathan Blecharczyk. Airbnb is headquartered in San Francisco. The company was initially called AirBed & Breakfast before shortening to Airbnb in March 2009.

Stripe, Inc. was established in 2010 in Palo Alto, California by Irish brothers Patrick Collison and John Collison. Patrick Collison serves as CEO and John Collison as President. Stripe maintains dual headquarters in South San Francisco, California, and Dublin, Ireland. Early investors included Elon Musk and Peter Thiel.

Slack is a team communication platform created by Stewart Butterfield. Slack was launched publicly in August 2013. Stewart Butterfield previously developed an unsuccessful online video game called Glitch. Salesforce acquired Slack in July 2021 for approximately $27.7 billion.

Hugging Face, Inc. is an American artificial intelligence company founded in 2016 by Clement Delangue, Julien Chaumond, and Thomas Wolf. Hugging Face is headquartered in Manhattan, New York City. Hugging Face is best known for its Transformers library for natural language processing.

Databricks, Inc. is a software company established in 2013 by Ali Ghodsi, Andy Konwinski, Ion Stoica, Matei Zaharia, Reynold Xin, Patrick Wendell, and Arsalan Tavakoli. Databricks is headquartered in San Francisco. The founders developed Apache Spark at UC Berkeley's AMPLab. Databricks acquired MosaicML in June 2023 for $1.4 billion.

Snap Inc. was founded on September 16, 2011, by Evan Spiegel, Bobby Murphy, and Reggie Brown. Snap Inc. is headquartered in Santa Monica, California. Snap was originally launched as Snapchat Inc. and rebranded to Snap Inc. in September 2016. Snap's flagship product is Snapchat. Snap also produces Spectacles smartglasses and Bitmoji.

Reddit was established in June 2005 by Steve Huffman and Alexis Ohanian. Conde Nast Publications acquired Reddit in October 2006. Steve Huffman currently serves as CEO of Reddit. Reddit went public on the New York Stock Exchange in March 2024.

LinkedIn was founded in December 2002 by Reid Hoffman and Eric Ly along with former PayPal team members. Microsoft acquired LinkedIn in December 2016 for $26.2 billion. Ryan Roslansky currently serves as CEO of LinkedIn. Reid Hoffman chairs the LinkedIn board.

Activision Blizzard was established on July 9, 2008, through a merger of Activision, Inc. and Vivendi Games. Bobby Kotick served as CEO. Activision Blizzard is headquartered in Santa Monica, California. The company produces Call of Duty, World of Warcraft, Overwatch, and Candy Crush Saga. Microsoft acquired Activision Blizzard for $68.7 billion; the acquisition completed on October 13, 2023.

Figma was founded in 2012 by Dylan Field and Evan Wallace, two computer science students at Brown University. Dylan Field received a Thiel Fellowship in 2012. Figma's first official release was on September 27, 2016. In September 2022, Adobe announced plans to acquire Figma for approximately $20 billion. Adobe and Figma abandoned the merger in December 2023.

Mistral AI is a French artificial intelligence company headquartered in Paris, founded on April 28, 2023 by Arthur Mensch, Guillaume Lample, and Timothee Lacroix. The founders met at Ecole Polytechnique. Arthur Mensch previously worked at Google DeepMind. Lample and Lacroix previously worked at Meta Platforms. Mistral AI develops large language models.

Palantir Technologies is a software company founded in 2003 by Peter Thiel, Alex Karp, Joe Lonsdale, Stephen Cohen, and Nathan Gettings. Palantir is headquartered in Denver, Colorado. Alex Karp serves as CEO of Palantir. Peter Thiel was a co-founder of PayPal.

Affirm Holdings is a financial technology company founded in 2012 by Max Levchin, Nathan Gettings, Jeffrey Kaditz, and Alex Rampell. Max Levchin serves as CEO of Affirm. Max Levchin was a co-founder of PayPal.

Microsoft owns several major subsidiaries acquired through history. Microsoft owns LinkedIn (acquired 2016). Microsoft owns GitHub (acquired 2018). Microsoft owns Activision Blizzard (acquired 2023). Microsoft owns Skype (acquired 2011 for $8.5 billion). Microsoft owns Mojang Studios, the maker of Minecraft (acquired 2014 for $2.5 billion). Microsoft owns ZeniMax Media (parent of Bethesda Softworks, acquired 2021 for $7.5 billion). Microsoft owns Nuance Communications (acquired 2022 for $19.7 billion).

Meta Platforms owns several subsidiaries. Meta Platforms owns Instagram (acquired 2012). Meta Platforms owns WhatsApp (acquired 2014). Meta Platforms owns Oculus VR (acquired 2014). Meta Platforms owns Beat Games, maker of Beat Saber (acquired 2019). Meta Platforms owns Within, maker of Supernatural (acquired 2022). Meta Platforms operates Facebook, Messenger, and Threads as in-house products.

Alphabet Inc. owns multiple subsidiaries. Alphabet owns Google. Alphabet owns YouTube via Google. Alphabet owns DeepMind. Alphabet owns Waymo. Alphabet owns Verily, a life sciences company. Alphabet owns Wing, a drone delivery company. Alphabet owns Calico, a longevity research company. Alphabet owns CapitalG, a growth equity firm.

Elon Musk has founded or co-founded several companies. Elon Musk co-founded Zip2 in 1995. Elon Musk founded X.com in 1999, which became PayPal. Elon Musk founded SpaceX in 2002. Elon Musk co-led Tesla's Series A funding in 2004. Elon Musk co-founded OpenAI in 2015. Elon Musk founded Neuralink in 2016. Elon Musk founded The Boring Company in 2016. Elon Musk founded xAI in 2023. Elon Musk acquired Twitter in 2022.
Neuralink is a neurotechnology company founded by Elon Musk in 2016. Neuralink is headquartered in Fremont, California. Neuralink develops implantable brain-computer interfaces.

The Boring Company is an infrastructure and tunnel construction company founded by Elon Musk in 2016. The Boring Company is headquartered in Bastrop, Texas. The Boring Company has built the Las Vegas Convention Center Loop.

Red Hat is an open-source software company founded in 1993 by Bob Young and Marc Ewing. Red Hat is headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina. Red Hat developed Red Hat Enterprise Linux. IBM acquired Red Hat in 2019 for $34 billion.
MosaicML was an AI company founded in 2021 by Naveen Rao and Hanlin Tang. MosaicML developed open-source generative AI tools. Databricks acquired MosaicML in 2023 for $1.4 billion.
Zappos is an online shoe and clothing retailer founded in 1999 by Nick Swinmurn. Tony Hsieh later became CEO. Amazon acquired Zappos in 2009 for $1.2 billion.
Twitch is a live streaming service founded in 2011 by Justin Kan and Emmett Shear, spun off from Justin.tv. Amazon acquired Twitch in 2014 for $970 million. Twitch is headquartered in San Francisco.
Audible is an audiobook and podcast service founded in 1995 by Don Katz. Amazon acquired Audible in 2008 for $300 million. Audible is headquartered in Newark, New Jersey.
Whole Foods Market is an American supermarket chain founded in 1980 by John Mackey, Renee Lawson Hardy, Craig Weller, and Mark Skiles. Amazon acquired Whole Foods in August 2017 for $13.7 billion.
Macromedia was a graphics, multimedia, and web development software company founded in 1992. Macromedia developed Flash. Adobe acquired Macromedia in 2005 for $3.4 billion.

Sun Microsystems was a technology company founded in 1982 by Vinod Khosla, Andy Bechtolsheim, Bill Joy, and Scott McNealy. Sun developed the Java programming language. Oracle acquired Sun Microsystems in 2010 for over $7 billion.
Skype was a telecommunications application founded in 2003 by Niklas Zennstrom and Janus Friis. eBay acquired Skype in 2005 for $2.6 billion. Microsoft acquired Skype in 2011 for $8.5 billion.
Minecraft is a sandbox video game developed by Mojang Studios. Mojang Studios was founded in 2009 by Markus Persson. Microsoft acquired Mojang Studios in 2014 for $2.5 billion.

Bethesda Softworks is a video game publisher founded in 1986 by Christopher Weaver. Bethesda is part of ZeniMax Media, founded in 1999 by Robert A. Altman. Microsoft acquired ZeniMax Media in 2021 for $7.5 billion. Bethesda is now owned by Microsoft.
Nuance Communications is a software company specializing in speech recognition. Microsoft acquired Nuance in 2022 for $19.7 billion.
Tableau Software is a data visualization company founded in 2003 by Christian Chabot, Pat Hanrahan, and Chris Stolte. Salesforce acquired Tableau in 2019 for $15.7 billion.
MuleSoft is an integration software company founded in 2006 by Ross Mason. Salesforce acquired MuleSoft in 2018 for $6.5 billion.
